# LaraKube CLI: AI-Native Kubernetes Orchestrator for Laravel

> This file provides a concise overview of LaraKube CLI for Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI crawlers.

## 🧱 Core Philosophy
LaraKube is a high-performance Kubernetes orchestrator built specifically for the Laravel ecosystem. It emphasizes architectural transparency, production-ready stability, and zero-config local development.

## 🤖 AI-Native DNA (Dual-MCP)
LaraKube implements a **Dual-MCP (Model Context Protocol)** architecture:
- **Local Mechanic (`larakube-cli`)**: Project-scoped server with 6 expert tools for local execution (scaffolding, patching, proxying).
- **Master Architect (`larakube-console`)**: Cluster-wide server with 9 fleet tools for global observability (logs, events, health monitoring).

## 🚀 The Scaling Roadmap ($6 Baseline)
LaraKube provides a professional graduation path for startups:
- **Local Dev ($0)**: 100% production parity via K3d.
- **Single-Node Hero ($6)**: High-performance 1GB VPS baseline using HostPort ingress and build offloading.
- **Enterprise Cluster**: Multi-node high availability on managed services (DOKS/GKE).

## 🛠 Top-Level Commands
- `new`: Scaffold a new LaraKube masterpiece.
- `init`: Adopt an existing Laravel project.
- `up`: Deploy infrastructure and application.
- `down`: Remove project resources with a 5-second volume cleanup cooldown.
- `uninstall --image`: Total project cleanup including manifests and Docker images.
- `traefik:*`: Dedicated suite for Managing the network layer (setup, logs, dashboard, restart, destroy).
- `doctor --ai`: Intelligent diagnostic and automated recovery.

## 🌍 Ecosystem Support
- **Frontend**: First-class support for **Starter Kits** using React, Vue, Svelte, and Livewire.
- **Databases**: Hardened stubs for PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, MongoDB, and Redis.
- **Services**: Integrated support for Horizon, Octane, Reverb, and Meilisearch.

## 📚 Full Context
For a complete synthesis of all documentation, see the [Master Context File](https://cli.larakube.app/llms-full.txt).

Full documentation available at: https://cli.larakube.app
